title: lupinus densiflorus subsp. austrocollium
intro: Lupinus densiflorus subsp. austrocollium, also known as the Southern Blue Lupine, is a subspecies of perennial flowering plant native to southwestern Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits also include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Lupinus densiflorus subsp. austrocollium can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to promote respiratory health and soothe coughs.
